overview:
The AWS Certified SysOps Administrator - Associate (SOA-C02) validates the ability to deploy, manage, and operate scalable and highly available systems on AWS. It focuses heavily on the operational aspects of the cloud, emphasizing monitoring, remediation, and automation to maintain system health and performance.

This exam is designed for individuals who have experience in the system administration interface of AWS. It is ideal for those who manage workloads, troubleshoot performance issues, and implement security controls. While there are no formal prerequisites, a strong understanding of core AWS services and general networking concepts is essential.
recertification_info: Recertify by retaking the current version of the SOA-C02 exam or by progressing to a professional-level certification such as the AWS Certified DevOps Engineer - Professional.
preparation_tips: Master CloudWatch Alarms, Logs, and Metrics, including how to create custom metrics and dashboards.
Deep dive into AWS Config for compliance monitoring and automated remediation using Systems Manager.
Practice deploying and managing Auto Scaling Groups, including scaling policies and health checks.
Understand the nuances of Route 53 routing policies (Weighted, Latency, Geolocation, Failover).
Get hands-on experience with AWS Systems Manager (SSM) for patching and remote command execution.
Study the Shared Responsibility Model specifically from an operational and security perspective.
Learn the differences between various backup strategies using AWS Backup and snapshots.
Understand VPC flow logs and how to use them to troubleshoot connectivity issues.
Explore AWS Organizations and Service Control Policies (SCPs) for multi-account governance.
Review cost optimization tools like Cost Explorer and AWS Compute Optimizer.
exam_day_tips: Carefully read the scenario; SysOps questions often include specific constraints like 'most cost-effective' or 'least operational effort'.
Manage your time strictly; if a question is too complex, flag it and move on to maintain momentum.
Eliminate obviously wrong answers first to increase your probability of choosing the correct one.
Pay close attention to keywords such as 'immediately', 'automatically', and 'permanently'.
Ensure your identification is ready and arrive early if taking the exam at a testing center.
recommended_experience: One year of hands-on experience implementing and managing AWS services in a production environment.
